trajectory.description: Observation: First iteration, empty workspace, full 10h/30it budget. Analysis: Need to quickly establish ERM baseline + measure per-iteration time using a standard, fast backbone (ResNet-50, 25M params, torchvision IMAGENET1K_V1) before committing to longer runs; must implement full training + predict pipeline that works offline. Decision: Create model.py (ResNet-50 head), train.py (custom Dataset from train.txt/val.txt, standard ImageNet aug+norm, DataParallel on 4 GPUs, 2 epochs, SGD, metric logging to required JSONL, best-val checkpoint), predict.py (exact eval contract, batched inference); run training then immediately submit_eval to obtain initial accuracy and timing data.

trajectory.description: Observation: ResNet-50 ERM baseline (2 epochs) reaches 65.8% source val acc but only 12.37% on Quickdraw target (expected large domain gap to line drawings); ~5.7min/epoch, pipeline fully functional with DataParallel on 4 GPUs. Analysis: Training converges on source but needs longer runs + domain-bridging augs (color jitter/grayscale to simulate sketches) + better schedule (lower LR + cosine) to improve OOD generalization without changing backbone yet. Decision: Increment to 10 epochs with lr=0.001, add RandomGrayscale+ColorJitter to train aug, switch to CosineAnnealingLR, log val acc to metrics.jsonl; run then submit_eval for new baseline before longer runs.

[eval_harness] Result: {'accuracy': 0.1801, 'balanced_accuracy': 0.1801}

trajectory.description: Observation: ResNet-50 ERM baseline reaches ~65.8% source val but only 12.37% target (iter1); iter2 with grayscale/colorjitter augs + cosine schedule gave slightly lower source val (64.55%) and worse target (11.18%). ~1h per 10 epochs on 4xGPU. Analysis: Large domain gap to Quickdraw line drawings not bridged well by current augs/backbone; transformer backbones often generalize better in DG papers. Decision: Switch to timm ViT-B/16 augreg_in1k (86M, paper-used supervised IN-1k transformer for DomainNet), AdamW optimizer (lr=1e-4), stronger augs (RandAugment + higher grayscale prob), 20 epochs with cosine LR (within rule), reduce train/predict batch to 128 for ViT memory safety, update model/predict/train accordingly then submit_eval for new baseline.

[eval_harness] Result: {'accuracy': 0.193, 'balanced_accuracy': 0.193}

trajectory.description: Observation: ViT-B/16 augreg reached 18.01% target acc (new best from 12.37%) with source val ~74% after 20 epochs; loss continues declining and val acc still rising slowly at end; ~3.8h for 20 epochs on 4xGPU. Analysis: DINOv2 (strong SSL backbone explicitly listed for DomainNet DG ablations) should yield more robust OOD features for the extreme sketch shift than supervised ViT; higher grayscale prob will better simulate Quickdraw line drawings while keeping other augs. Decision: Switch to DINOv2-B with linear head (86M params, fits limit), edit train for 10 epochs (fits ~2h remaining time), slightly higher grayscale p=0.7 + magnitude=10, lower LR=8e-5 for SSL backbone, then train + submit_eval immediately for updated baseline.

trajectory.error_summary: [TIMEOUT] Command exceeded 1708s limit. Consider reducing epochs or using a smaller model.
trajectory.description: Observation: DINOv2 linear probe reaches new best 19.3% target acc (source val 74.5% still rising slowly at end of 10ep); each epoch ~13.4min so ~2ep possible in remaining 28.5min. Analysis: Further optimization on current strong SSL backbone + continued high grayscale aug should help bridge to Quickdraw line drawings without new model. Decision: Update train.py to resume from best.pt (load module state, new AdamW+cosine at low LR=2e-5 for 2 epochs), keep strong augs, run training then submit_eval on updated checkpoint; no predict.py change needed.
